message:
refactor: improve cache pressure calculation, extract evidence printing, enhance E2E mount robustness

ffs-block: Replace floating-point memory pressure ratios with safe integer
fractions (target_fraction() returning numerator/denominator tuples) to avoid
rounding issues in cache pressure calculations. Add target_capacity() helper
with saturating arithmetic for overflow safety. Extract 88-line flush daemon
cycle body into dedicated run_flush_daemon_cycle() method for readability and
testability. Fix test ordering: start flush daemon after filling cache with
dirty blocks so daemon actually observes dirty state.

ffs-cli: Add env_bool() parser for FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT environment variable
supporting 1/0/true/false/yes/no/on/off (case-insensitive) to handle rootless
FUSE setups where implicit allow_other causes permission failures. Extract
massive match block from print_evidence_record() into 13 specialized payload
handler functions (print_corruption_payload, print_repair_payload,
print_scrub_cycle_payload, etc.) using .as_ref() for safe optional access.
Add print_evidence_record_event_payload() dispatcher.

ffs-repair: Tighten codec test to directly assert seed derivation invariant
(enc_g0.seed != enc_g1.seed) instead of comparing symbol data which may
coincidentally match. Widen recovery and storage test assertions to accept
valid error path variants (symbol-read failures OR no valid repair generation)
for robustness to implementation details.

E2E (scripts/e2e): Default FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 for ext4 RW smoke test. Add
FFS_CLI_BIN variable to use pre-built binary instead of cargo run. Enhance
wait_for_mount_ready() to check both mountpoint -q and log file for FUSE INIT
response. Add post-mount permission check with explicit e2e_skip diagnostic
for fusermount3 permission denied. Document new environment knobs in README.

beads: Update bd-2s4.5 with crash harness progress and mount-permission gating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2s4.2","title":"Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations","description":"# Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations

## GOAL
Enable writing and removing extended attributes through the FUSE mount interface.

## BACKGROUND
listxattr and getxattr are already implemented (read-only). This bead adds the write side:
- setxattr: Create or replace an extended attribute
- removexattr: Delete an extended attribute

Requires inline xattr space management (may need to promote to external block if inline space exhausted).

## DELIVERABLES
1. FrankenFuse::setxattr() handler
2. FrankenFuse::removexattr() handler
3. Inline xattr space management (check if fits, promote if needed)
4. Integration with MVCC transaction wrapping

## TESTS
1. Set xattr on file, read it back
2. Replace existing xattr with larger value
3. Remove xattr, verify gone
4. Overflow inline space → external block (or ENOSPC if not supported)
5. EROFS on read-only mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. POSIX xattr semantics preserved
2. Transaction-wrapped for crash safety
3. No data corruption on inline/external boundary","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:29.024787494Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.398307753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.3","title":"Implement crash recovery on mount after unclean shutdown","description":"# Implement crash recovery on mount after unclean shutdown

## GOAL
When FrankenFS mounts a filesystem image that was not cleanly unmounted (crash, SIGKILL, power loss), it must detect the unclean state and perform recovery before serving any requests.

## BACKGROUND
Unclean shutdown can leave:
- Uncommitted MVCC transactions (partial writes in version chains)
- Stale RaptorQ symbols (dirty groups not refreshed)
- Incomplete journal transactions (JBD2 write path)
- Dirty inode/block caches not flushed to disk

Without crash recovery, mounting after a crash could expose partially-written data, stale versions, or inconsistent metadata.

## DESIGN

### Unclean Shutdown Detection
- Superblock dirty flag: set on mount, cleared on clean unmount
- If dirty flag is set on mount: unclean shutdown detected
- Journal recovery: check for uncommitted journal transactions

### Recovery Steps (ordered)
1. Journal replay: apply committed but unflushed journal transactions
2. MVCC rollback: abort any in-flight transactions (no commit record = abort)
3. Version chain cleanup: remove uncommitted versions from chains
4. RaptorQ symbol validation: mark all groups as needing re-verification
5. Trigger immediate scrub of metadata-critical groups (superblock, GDT, inode tables)
6. Clear superblock dirty flag after recovery completes
7. Log all recovery actions to evidence ledger

### Integration Points
- ffs-cli mount command: detect unclean state before serving FUSE
- Journal replay (bd-1xe.4): already handles ext4 journal replay
- MVCC subsystem: needs abort/rollback API for in-flight transactions
- Scrub daemon: needs priority queue for post-recovery metadata verification

### Error Handling
- If recovery fails (journal corrupt, metadata unreadable): refuse to mount, log detailed error
- If recovery partial (some transactions recovered, others not): mount read-only, log warning
- Never silently ignore unclean state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Unclean shutdown detected via superblock dirty flag
- [ ] Journal transactions replayed correctly on recovery mount
- [ ] In-flight MVCC transactions aborted cleanly
- [ ] RaptorQ symbols re-verified after crash recovery
- [ ] Evidence ledger records all recovery actions
- [ ] Mount refuses if recovery fails completely
- [ ] Read-only fallback if recovery is partial
- [ ] Unit tests: mock unclean state, verify recovery steps","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:08.758264993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:32:45.652294709Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:32:45.652258782Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","fuse","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:25.889600663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:09.698886837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:08.636193258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:08.758264993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":80,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.4","title":"E2E test: mount, write 10000 files, unmount, remount, verify all files","description":"# E2E test: mount, write 10000 files, unmount, remount, verify all files

## GOAL
Verify that FrankenFS correctly persists data across mount/unmount cycles. This is the fundamental persistence correctness test.

## TEST SCENARIO

### Phase 1: Write
1. Create fresh 512MB ext4 image with FrankenFS
2. Mount via FUSE (read-write mode)
3. Create directory tree: 100 directories, 100 files each = 10000 files
4. Each file: deterministic content based on path (e.g., BLAKE3(path) repeated to fill 4KB)
5. Compute BLAKE3 checksum manifest for all 10000 files
6. Unmount cleanly

### Phase 2: Verify
7. Remount the same image (read-only mode)
8. Walk entire directory tree, verify all 10000 files present
9. Read each file, verify BLAKE3 checksum matches manifest
10. Verify directory structure (ls each directory, check file count)
11. Unmount

### Phase 3: Read-Write Cycle
12. Remount read-write
13. Modify 1000 random files (new deterministic content)
14. Delete 500 random files
15. Create 500 new files
16. Update manifest
17. Unmount, remount, verify updated manifest

### Timing Requirements
- Phase 1: < 30s for 10000 files on SSD
- Phase 2: < 15s for verification
- Phase 3: < 20s for modifications + verification

### Failure Modes to Detect
- File content corruption (checksum mismatch)
- Missing files (not persisted to disk)
- Extra files (stale data from previous test)
- Directory entry corruption (wrong file count)
- Permission/ownership corruption

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 10000 files survive mount/unmount cycle with correct content
- [ ] Directory structure exactly matches expected layout
- [ ] Read-write-modify cycle preserves all changes
- [ ] Test is deterministic (same seed = same result)
- [ ] Test completes in < 120s total
- [ ] Test passes in CI without root (FUSE with allow_other or user mount)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:23.300578406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:42:23.858009121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:42:23.857987540Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fuse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:12.479357004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:23.300578406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:11.675408697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2s4.5","title":"E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency","description":"# E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency

## GOAL
Verify that FrankenFS maintains consistency guarantees after an unclean shutdown (SIGKILL during active writes). The filesystem must be mountable, crash recovery must work, and all committed data must be intact.

## TEST SCENARIO

### Phase 1: Setup
1. Create fresh 256MB ext4 image
2. Mount via FUSE, write baseline dataset (500 files, checksums recorded)
3. fsync all files, verify baseline is durable
4. Begin continuous write workload (background thread writing new files)

### Phase 2: Crash
5. After 2 seconds of active writing, send SIGKILL to FUSE process
6. Record which files had fsync called vs which were in-flight

### Phase 3: Recovery
7. Remount the filesystem image
8. Crash recovery should trigger automatically (journal replay, MVCC rollback)
9. Record evidence ledger output

### Phase 4: Verification
10. All baseline files (pre-crash) must be intact with correct checksums
11. Files that had fsync completed must be intact
12. Files that were in-flight at SIGKILL time: acceptable to be missing or truncated, but:
    - Must NOT contain garbage data
    - Must NOT corrupt other files
    - Directory must be consistent (no dangling entries)
13. No filesystem-level corruption (fsck-equivalent check passes)

### Invariants
- Atomicity: a file write either fully committed or fully absent
- No cross-file corruption: crash during file A write must never affect file B
- Metadata consistency: directory entries match existing inodes
- Journal consistency: no partial journal transactions visible

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Filesystem mounts after SIGKILL without errors
- [ ] All pre-crash baseline files intact with correct checksums
- [ ] All fsync'd files intact
- [ ] In-flight files are either complete or cleanly absent (no partial garbage)
- [ ] No cross-file corruption
- [ ] Evidence ledger records crash recovery actions
- [ ] Test is reproducible (deterministic write pattern)
- [ ] Test completes in < 60s","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveSummit","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:33:04.848447377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","e2e","fuse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:10.730235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:13.504998589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: runtime_backpressure
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.5 Graceful Degradation + 10.3 Learning-Augmented Online + 12.13 Change-point
EV score: 16 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:20Z"},{"id":220,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"OliveSummit","text":"Implemented deterministic SIGKILL crash/recovery harness in scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh and documented controls in scripts/e2e/README.md. New crash phase now: (1) writes+fsyncs configurable baseline dataset (default 500 files), (2) runs continuous in-flight writer with event log, (3) SIGKILLs mount daemon after configurable runtime, (4) remounts RO, (5) verifies baseline checksums + fsync durability invariants + non-fsync clean-prefix/no-garbage behavior, and (6) checks remount log for crash-recovery evidence. Validation run: bash -n passes. rch offloaded gates attempted; cargo check/clippy/test all fail remotely with pre-existing libc resolution conflict from /dp/asupersync (libc=0.2.180) vs ffs-core (libc ^0.2.181).","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:33:04Z"}]}

Run skipped at mount phase due fuse3 allow_other policy (user_allow_other not enabled in /etc/fuse.conf), with explicit diagnostic captured in artifacts/e2e/20260216_104540_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log and mount_rw_mnt_rw.log.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:46:59Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"OliveSummit","text":"Added mount-permission phase gate to scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh: explicit SKIP when fusermount reports Permission denied / Operation not permitted. Artifacts: artifacts/e2e/20260216_105229_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log and artifacts/e2e/20260216_105229_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/mount_rw_mnt_rw.log. This indicates remaining ext4 RW functional gap beyond environment gating.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:53:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Production FUSE Runtime epic (bd-2s4). Currently the epic has E2E tests (bd-2s4.4, bd-2s4.5, bd-3en1) but NO dedicated unit tests for the dispatch layer, lifecycle management, or errno mapping.

## TEST PLAN

### 1. Dispatch Tests
- Thread pool spawns correct number of threads
- Concurrent requests are dispatched without deadlock
- Request timeout fires and returns EIO
- Queue depth metrics are updated correctly

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Mount initializes all subsystems in correct order
- Unmount flushes dirty data before teardown
- SIGTERM triggers clean unmount sequence
- Double-unmount is idempotent (no panic)
- Mount with invalid image returns descriptive error

### 3. Errno Mapping Tests
- Every FfsError variant maps to correct errno
- Unknown errors map to EIO
- Cancelled maps to EINTR
- CorruptData maps to EIO
- NoSpace maps to ENOSPC
- PermissionDenied maps to EACCES

### 4. State Tests
- FsOps state is Send + Sync (compile-time test)
- Concurrent getattr calls do not race
- Cache access under lock is bounded (no starvation)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. All tests listed above
2. Total: ~20 unit tests
3. All deterministic, no I/O (mock BlockDevice)

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full context (operation, expected, actual)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All dispatch, lifecycle, errno, and state tests pass
2. No flaky tests (deterministic)
3. Coverage of all FUSE op errno mappings","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:24.844808978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:49.972174278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-cebm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.086927457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2t1","title":"MVCC: Specify API + invariants (TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot) and phase plan (FCW -> SSI)","description":"Goal: make MVCC semantics unambiguous before integrating with filesystem operations.

Deliverables:
- Document: what a Snapshot means (visibility window), when it is taken, and how it is used for reads.
- Document: transaction lifecycle (begin -> stage writes -> commit/abort) and what is allowed concurrently.
- Phase plan:
  - Phase A: First-committer-wins (FCW) conflict check at commit.
  - Phase B: Add optional SSI tracking (read sets + rw-antidependencies) where needed.

Acceptance:
- ffs-mvcc public API is stable enough that ffs-fuse can build on it.

 {"id":"bd-2tq","title":"Semantics: Define a minimal VFS operations trait for FUSE/harness","description":"Goal: define the internal interface FUSE and harness call.

Deliverables:
- A trait (e.g. FsOps) with methods needed for read-only mount:
  - getattr(inode)
  - lookup(parent,name)
  - readdir(inode)
  - read(inode,offset,len)
- Types for file attributes and directory entries.

Acceptance:
- ffs-fuse can implement the fuser::Filesystem methods by delegating to this trait.
- ext4 implementation can live behind this interface (and later btrfs too).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:14.391246994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:29:28.832170053Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:29:28.832148523Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["semantics"]}
